verdict_reason: The voice for 2.33 holds the chapter's directive register and is appropriately sharp: 'The verse does not condemn quitting a job. It condemns quitting a duty mid-execution when the only person who can carry it forward is the one walking out.' That is precise stern voice — Krishna closing an escape route — without being moralistic. Continuity with 2.31 and 2.32 is good; the karma-yoga block voice is internally consistent. Sentence-length variance is healthy. PASS.
